brand.name: Top Tier Coffees
brand.tagline: Small-Batch Roasted to a Higher Standard

offer.what: Small-batch roasted specialty coffee (50+ varieties: single-origin, blends, flavored, functional), premium teas (8+ varieties), K-cup pods, and curated sample packs — shipped same day as roasting
offer.coreOutcome: Fresh, craft coffee you can actually taste the difference in — no stale supermarket bags, no mystery sourcing
offer.pricePoint: $19.99–$23.99 per bag one-time; sample packs $21.99; K-cup 60-pack $59.99; subscription available (flexible frequency, cancel anytime, no fees)
offer.commitment: One-off purchase or recurring subscription; subscription requires 48-hour cancellation notice before next shipment
leadMagnets: Email newsletter signup for exclusive offers and new collection announcements
Sample packs ($21.99) as low-risk trial/exploration entry point

audience.icp: Home coffee enthusiast, 25–45, who values freshness and craft over convenience — owns specialty brewing gear, fed up with stale grocery-store bags, willing to pay $20–$24 for a bag they can trust
painPoints: Stale, over-roasted coffee from grocery store or big chains
No transparency into roast date or sourcing
Hard to discover new varieties without committing to a large bag
Subscription lock-in anxiety — fear of being stuck or overcharged
segments: Single-origin aficionados — want traceability and terroir
Blend/flavored casual drinkers — want consistent, enjoyable daily cup
K-cup convenience buyers — need pod format for office or ease
Gift buyers — looking for premium gifting option for coffee lovers

salesCycle.length: Same day to 2–3 days (low-consideration DTC e-commerce purchase)
salesCycle.trial: Sample packs ($21.99) serve as a 6-variety tasting kit; no free trial but low price point reduces risk
salesCycle.closeMechanism: Self-serve e-commerce checkout (Shopify); free US shipping removes final friction
salesCycle.topObjection: Is this actually fresher/better than what I can find locally, and is the price justified?

ahaMoment: First sip of a freshly roasted bag received within days of roasting — 'I can actually taste the difference' (same-day-roasted freshness is the activation proof point)

retention.recurring: Subscription model — flexible frequency, skip/pause up to 3 months, swap products anytime, cancel anytime with no fees
retention.expansion: Upgrade to larger bag, add tea products, explore new single-origins, gift subscriptions to others
retention.churnRisk: Missing the 48-hour cancel window causing frustration; discovering a preferred local roaster; price sensitivity if quality difference isn't felt quickly

lifecycleGaps: Welcome & Onboarding — no evidence of a post-purchase sequence reinforcing freshness story or guiding first brew
Sample Pack → Subscription conversion — no nurture between trial purchase and recurring upsell
Lead-Magnet Nurture — newsletter signup has no visible drip sequence to convert browsers
Value Newsletter — no active content/education cadence visible (blog appears inactive)
Renewal / Re-engagement — no evidence of win-back for lapsed subscribers or one-time buyers
Referral / Advocacy — no visible referral program despite strong product-driven word-of-mouth potential
toneVoice: Confident and aspirational without being elitist. Educational about craft/sourcing but never pretentious. Direct, clean copy — lets the product do the talking. Warm and honest (founder-accessible: dave@toptiercoffees.com visible publicly).
compliance: Standard CAN-SPAM / GDPR compliance required. No HIPAA, financial, or legal vertical constraints. Food product — no health claims. Subscription emails must include clear cancellation instructions per FTC guidelines.
